---
title: "HiTOP Scale Definitions"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{HiTOP Scale Definitions}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include=FALSE, message=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
library(dplyr)
library(hitop)
```

The sections below contain the brief and client-facing definitions for each scale and its respective subscales. Use the sidebar menu to navigate directly to specific scales.

```{r, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
data_df <- hitopsr_definitions
scales <- unique(data_df$Scale)

for (s in scales) {
  scale_data <- data_df |> dplyr::filter(Scale == s)

  # Main Scale Heading
  cat("\n\n## ", s, "\n\n", sep = "")

  # 1. Main Scale Cards Layout
  main_def <- scale_data |> dplyr::filter(is.na(Subscale))
  if (nrow(main_def) > 0) {
    brief_text <- if (!is.na(main_def$Brief[1])) {
      main_def$Brief[1]
    } else {
      "<i>Definition not available.</i>"
    }
    client_text <- if (!is.na(main_def$Client[1])) {
      main_def$Client[1]
    } else {
      "<i>Definition not available.</i>"
    }

    cat('<div class="row g-3 mb-4">\n')

    # Clinician Card (Using bg-body-tertiary for dynamic dark-mode compatibility)
    cat('<div class="col-md-6">\n')
    cat(
      '<div class="card h-100 border-0 shadow-sm border-start border-primary border-4 bg-body-tertiary">\n'
    )
    cat('<div class="card-body py-3">\n')
    cat(
      '<h6 class="text-primary text-uppercase fw-bold small mb-1">Clinician Brief</h6>\n'
    )
    cat('<p class="card-text text-body-secondary">', brief_text, '</p>\n')
    cat('</div>\n')
    cat('</div>\n')
    cat('</div>\n')

    # Client Card
    cat('<div class="col-md-6">\n')
    cat(
      '<div class="card h-100 border-0 shadow-sm border-start border-success border-4 bg-body-tertiary">\n'
    )
    cat('<div class="card-body py-3">\n')
    cat(
      '<h6 class="text-success text-uppercase fw-bold small mb-1">Client-Facing</h6>\n'
    )
    cat('<p class="card-text text-body-secondary">', client_text, '</p>\n')
    cat('</div>\n')
    cat('</div>\n')
    cat('</div>\n')

    cat('</div>\n\n')
  }

  # 2. Subscales Layout
  sub_defs <- scale_data |> dplyr::filter(!is.na(Subscale))
  if (nrow(sub_defs) > 0) {
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(sub_defs))) {
      # Subscale Heading
      cat("\n\n### ", sub_defs$Subscale[i], "\n\n", sep = "")

      sub_brief <- if (!is.na(sub_defs$Brief[i])) {
        sub_defs$Brief[i]
      } else {
        "<i>Definition not available.</i>"
      }
      sub_client <- if (!is.na(sub_defs$Client[i])) {
        sub_defs$Client[i]
      } else {
        "<i>Definition not available.</i>"
      }

      # Using bg-body-secondary container for adaptive contrast nesting
      cat(
        '<div class="p-3 mb-4 bg-body-secondary rounded-3 border-start border-secondary border-3">\n'
      )
      cat(
        '<div class="mb-2"><strong>Clinician Brief:</strong> <span class="text-body-secondary">',
        sub_brief,
        '</span></div>\n'
      )
      cat(
        '<div><strong>Client-Facing:</strong> <span class="text-body-secondary">',
        sub_client,
        '</span></div>\n'
      )
      cat('</div>\n\n')
    }
  }

  # Dynamic thematic divider line
  cat("\n\n<hr class=\"my-5\">\n\n")
}
```
